general_advisory: The mean maximum daily temperature was 37.3°C and mean minimum daily temperature was 24.8°C of the Cuttack district during the last week. The district received 0.2 mm rainfall during the last seven days. Transplanting of summer paddy is completed. Sowing of summer pulses, oilseed and vegetables are also under progress. Harvesting of rabi crops like Mustard, Groundnut, Mung, Biri and Kulthi are completed. Crop coverage till date is 99% of the total programme area. Overall crop condition is Normal.

advisory: In paddy crop, Panicle initiation, Panicle emergence, flowering and milking stages are the critical stages. Water deficit during these stages should be avoided.
Cover the rows with dry leaves in Sugarcane. Top-dress with 4% potassium to protect crop from water scarcity during summer.
Irrigate the crop frequently during hot months to manage early shoot borer in Sugarcane.
High temperature increases the water requirement of maize crop. Irrigate the crop. Avoid stagnant water in the field for more than six hours.
High temperature in day hours may lead to wilting in tomato. Apply 1g Streptocyclin in 10 liter water in root zone.
Install pheromone traps for Brinjal shoot and fruit borer control.
Spray neem-based pesticide for pest infestation. Use chemical control if needed.
Mulch coconut leaves 1.8 m around a coconut tree to reduce water loss.
Cover with paper caps on the newly planted vegetable seedlings during day time.
Check the migration of caterpillars in Black gram. Use appropriate insecticides for control.
Check the migration of caterpillars in Green gram. Use appropriate insecticides for control.
After harvesting, dry the bulbs well to store for a longer period.
To control mango hoppers, spray appropriate insecticides. Use smoke below the plants to reduce pest population.
